senses.definition: A genus of extinct sea turtles from the Late Cretaceous, including the species Protostega gigas.
examples: Protostega gigas was one of the largest sea turtles of its time.
Fossils attributed to Protostega have been found in North America.

senses.definition: A family of extinct marine turtles from the Crétaceous period.
examples: Protostegidae includes some of the largest ancient sea turtles.
Fossils of Protostegidae indicate a cuirass-like shell structure.

senses.definition: A simple, solid core of xylem in some plant stems or roots, surrounded by phloem.
examples: Protostele is a primitive vascular arrangement found in some ancient plants.
In a protostele, the xylem forms a central cylinder.

senses.definition: Plural of protostele; multiple plants or stems with this primitive vascular arrangement.
examples: Ferns may show protosteles in their primitive vascular systems.
Protosteles are discussed in contrast to more complex stele types.

senses.definition: Relating to or composed of protostele; having a simple solid core of xylem.
examples: Protostelic stems were common in early land plants.
The protostelic arrangementlimited the vascular complexity of the specimen.

senses.definition: An animal whose mouth develops from the first opening in the embryo; a major group of bilaterally symmetric animals (Protostomia).
examples: Most mollusks and arthropods are protostomes.
Protostomes show spiral cleavage during early development.

senses.definition: The members of the animal group Protostomia; animals whose mouth forms from the opening in early development.
examples: Protostomes include insects, snails, and worms.
Study of protostomes helps explain animal evolution.

senses.definition: A chemical element symbolically named in hypothetical or historical contexts; may refer to a nonstandard or obsolete term.
examples: Protostrontium appears in some antiquated catalogs.
The term protostrontium is rarely used in modern chemistry.

senses.definition: A hypothetical or historic chemical salt of sulfuric acid, potentially spelled protosulphate; may appear in older texts.
examples: Protosulphate is not commonly used in modern chemistry nomenclature.

senses.definition: A historical or theoretical sulfide compound; an archaic or nonstandard term.
examples: Some old texts mention protosulphide compounds.

senses.definition: A rare or technical term possibly used in scholarly contexts to describe a primitive or early syntonous feature.
examples: Protosyntonose is used in some niche texts.

senses.definition: A genus of large, extinct land organisms from the Ordovician to Devonian, known from fossil impressions; often described as a giant fungal-like organism or algal-algal fossil.
examples: Prototaxites fossils are some of the oldest large terrestrial organisms.
Prototaxites sp. indicates ancient, prolific growth in forests.

senses.definition: A genus of algae, some species of which can cause disease in humans or animals; also used in microbiology contexts.
examples: Prototheca species are unusual algae that can contaminate samples.
Infection with Prototheca is rare but reported in immunocompromised patients.

senses.definition: Relating to Prototheca or infections caused by Prototheca.
examples: Protothecal infections are uncommon but notable in medical case reports.

senses.definition: A term used in linguistic or philosophical contexts to denote a primitive or earliest theme or motive.
examples: The prototheme of the narrative appears early in the text.

senses.definition: An extinct semi-marsupial mammal from the Mesozoic era; an early monotreme-like mammal.
examples: Protothere fossils help scientists understand early mammal evolution.

senses.definition: A subclass of mammals including monotremes; a primitive mammal grouping.
examples: Prototheria comprises the monotremes like the platypus and echidna.

senses.definition: A member of Prototheria; monotreme mammal such as platypus or echidna.
examples: The platypus is a prototherian.

senses.definition: Plural of prototherian; multiple monotreme mammals.
examples: Prototherians include the platypus and the echidna.

senses.definition: A hypothetical or proposed early form of titanium; may appear in speculative or historical contexts.
examples: Prototitanium is used in discussions of early metal chemistry.

senses.definition: A hypothetical early member of the group Tracheata; used in historical/phylogenetic contexts.
examples: Prototracheata appears in discussions of insect evolution.

senses.definition: An imagined or historical term for someone who betrays early allies; used in fiction or historical context.
examples: In the saga, the prototraitor changes sides at a critical moment.

senses.definition: In zoology, the first larval band or trophic ring in certain trochophore larvae; a developmental feature.
examples: The prototroch is visible in early larval stages.

senses.definition: Relating to prototroch or to the trochophore larval stage.
examples: Prototrochal development is studied in embryology.

senses.definition: An organism capable of synthesizing all its required nutrients from simple inorganic substances; a self-sufficient organism.
examples: A prototroph can grow on minimal media without added nutrients.
